text.title: Trunk-Based Development Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the trunk-based-dev-patterns methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Branches MUST live less than 24 hours. Rebase onto main before opening the PR; squash on merge so trunk history stays linear.
rule.rationale: Long-lived branches accumulate conflicts and diverge from trunk, defeating the trunk-based model. Squash merge keeps history searchable.

rule.statement: Each flag MUST map to exactly one task and a cleanup ticket filed at flag birth. Reusing a flag for unrelated work is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Flag reuse blurs ownership; cleanup tickets filed later are filed never. Tracking at birth keeps inventory bounded.

rule.statement: Flag names MUST follow <area>.<verb>_<thing> with positive verbs (use_, enable_, show_). Boolean-negative names (disable_, no_, hide_) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Negative naming inverts logic when renamed mid-rollout, producing off-by-one bugs in agent-generated code.

rule.statement: Dark launches MUST run at < 1% traffic for at least one full diurnal cycle (24h) before scaling. Direct flip to 100% is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Low-traffic canary catches bugs safely; scaling too fast masks bugs that only appear at scale.

rule.statement: A PR that broke main MUST be reverted by bot, not by humans. Fix-forward in a fresh PR.
rule.rationale: Manual reverts are slow and partial; bot reverts are deterministic. Fix-forward keeps blame clean.

rule.statement: CI MUST complete in under 10 minutes. Slow CI breaks the daily-merge cadence.
rule.rationale: Developers batch pushes when feedback is slow; rapid feedback is what makes trunk-based safe.

rule.statement: Direct pushes to main MUST be forbidden for humans; only automation may push. Linear history + signed commits MUST be required.
rule.rationale: Direct pushes bypass CI gates and introduce merge commits; requiring PRs ensures every change passes review + gates.
